guidance.agency_name: New Jersey Department of Health / New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection
guidance.repeat_testing_guidance: New Jersey private well owners should use PWTA timing, local conditions, and NJDOH or NJDEP guidance rather than a single Sharp v1 retest cadence. The route should keep certified-lab collection and follow-up context visible.
guidance.benchmark_notes: New Jersey has state PFAS drinking-water standards and PWTA-related private well context, but private wells remain owner-managed. Outputs should stay reference-based and should not be framed as household compliance findings.

profile.primary_reference_label: New Jersey PFAS drinking-water standards used with PWTA private-well context
profile.summary: New Jersey already regulates PFOA, PFOS, and PFNA in drinking water and requires PFAS testing under the Private Well Testing Act. For Sharp v1, New Jersey gives unusually direct private-well reference context for covered analytes, but the route should still keep the current state-to-federal transition and certified-lab workflow visible.

benchmarks.label: New Jersey MCL for PFOA
benchmarks.note: New Jersey currently enforces a 14 ppt state MCL for PFOA while state-federal transition work continues.

benchmarks.label: New Jersey MCL for PFOS
benchmarks.note: New Jersey currently enforces a 13 ppt state MCL for PFOS.

benchmarks.label: New Jersey MCL for PFNA
benchmarks.note: New Jersey currently enforces a 13 ppt state MCL for PFNA and includes PFNA in PWTA-required PFAS testing.

profile.private_well_use_note: Use New Jersey's PFOA, PFOS, and PFNA standards as reference context for covered analytes and keep PWTA-certified sampling and local follow-up in view. The route should not present a private well as if it were a regulated community system.

entry_decision_input.summary: No direct well test is available yet, so the engine should route the household to a state-guided lab and sampling workflow before any product comparison.
